preamble: The shift from open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] to nasalized open central unrounded vowel [ã] involves both nasalization and lowering of the vowel tongue position.

examples.note: French historical development saw open-mid vowels nasalize and lower before nasal consonants. E.g., 'dent' [dɛ̃nt] > [dã].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
